data.title: XVIVO’s U.S. PRESERVE Trial Shows Strong One-Year Outcomes for Heart Preservation Technology
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An announcement from Xvivo Perfusion AB ( (SE:XVIVO) ) is now available.

XVIVO Perfusion AB reported preliminary one-year follow-up data from the U.S. PRESERVE trial evaluating its hypothermic oxygenated perfusion heart technology, Heart Assist Transport, in extended criteria donor and donation after circulatory death hearts. The study enrolled 141 transplant recipients across 14 U.S. centers and met its pre-specified primary endpoints, with a 92.1% overall success rate at 30 days, 91.4% patient survival at one year, and a 7.9% incidence of severe primary graft dysfunction at 24 hours.

The median out-of-body time for donor hearts was 291 minutes and nearly three-quarters of hearts were preserved for more than four hours, underscoring the system’s potential to mitigate ischemic time constraints compared with standard static cold storage. XVIVO’s management framed the results as an important step in advancing organ preservation and broadening the use of higher-risk donor hearts, and said the company plans to submit the PRESERVE data to the U.S. Food and Drug Administration for review later this year, a move that could be pivotal for its U.S. market ambitions if the investigational device ultimately gains approval.

The most recent analyst rating on (SE:XVIVO) stock is a Buy with a SEK225.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Xvivo Perfusion AB stock, see the SE:XVIVO Stock Forecast page.

More about Xvivo Perfusion AB

XVIVO Perfusion AB is a Swedish medical technology company focused on organ preservation solutions that extend the life of all major organs for transplantation. Founded in 1998 and listed on Nasdaq Stockholm under the ticker XVIVO, the company supports transplant teams globally through specialized technologies and research aimed at expanding the pool of viable donor organs and improving transplant outcomes.

Average Trading Volume: 151,164

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: SEK9.37B

data.title: Unidata Corrects Governance Filings Ahead of Board of Statutory Auditors Appointment
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions

Unidata S.P.A. ( (IT:UD) ) has shared an update.

Unidata S.p.A., an Italian telecommunications, cloud and IoT operator founded in 1985, operates an 8,500 km fiber network, wireless infrastructure and a proprietary data center to deliver ultra-broadband FTTH connectivity, VoIP, cloud services and custom solutions to over 31,000 customers. The company is also active in Internet of Things applications for home automation and smart cities, and its shares trade on the Euronext Milan – STAR Segment of Borsa Italiana.

The company has reissued its remuneration report and the shareholder Uninvest S.r.l.’s list for appointing the Board of Statutory Auditors after correcting previously incomplete or non-viewable information. By updating these governance documents and making them publicly available through its registered office, an authorized storage mechanism and its website, Unidata seeks to ensure regulatory compliance and transparency for shareholders ahead of corporate decision-making on executive pay and board oversight composition.

The most recent analyst rating on (IT:UD) stock is a Buy with a EUR4.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Unidata S.P.A. stock, see the IT:UD Stock Forecast page.

More about Unidata S.P.A.

Unidata S.p.A. is an Italian telecommunications, cloud and IoT operator founded in 1985 and still led by its original three partners. Leveraging an 8,500 km and growing fiber network, a wireless network and a proprietary data center, it serves more than 31,000 business, wholesale and residential customers with ultra-broadband FTTH connectivity, VoIP, cloud and tailored solutions, and develops IoT offerings for home automation and smart city applications.

The company, listed on the Euronext Milan – STAR Segment of Borsa Italiana under ISIN IT0005573065, positions itself as a high-reliability infrastructure player in Italy’s digital transformation landscape. Its integrated telecoms and cloud platform underpins its competitive stance in both traditional connectivity and emerging IoT-driven services.

Art’s-Way Manufacturing Co ( (ARTW) ) has shared an update.

On April 21, 2026, Art’s-Way Manufacturing Co., Inc. held its 2026 Annual Meeting of Stockholders, where shareholders elected five directors to the board and ratified the appointment of Eide Bailly LLP as independent auditor for the fiscal year ending November 30, 2026. Investors also approved an increase of 500,000 shares under the 2020 Equity Incentive Plan and endorsed, on an advisory basis, the compensation of the company’s named executive officers, signaling continued shareholder support for current governance, incentive structures, and leadership direction.

These actions strengthen Art’s-Way’s governance framework by confirming board composition and auditor oversight, while the expanded equity pool enhances the company’s ability to attract and retain key talent in a competitive agricultural equipment market. The advisory approval of executive pay indicates general shareholder alignment with management’s strategic approach, potentially supporting stability in the company’s operations and long-term planning.

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, ARTW is a Neutral.

The score is held back primarily by weak and volatile cash generation (negative TTM operating and free cash flow) despite improving profitability and a healthier, de-risking balance sheet. Technicals are supportive with the price above major moving averages, while valuation is middling based on a moderate P/E and no visible dividend yield.

The latest update is out from First Guaranty Bancshares ( (FGBI) ).

First Guaranty Bancshares, Inc. elected Betsy K. Hood to its Board of Directors on April 16, 2026, adding a leader with more than 25 years of experience in the retail automotive industry. Hood, Dealer Principal of Hood Automotive Group and president of Southern Dealers Mechanical Breakdown Insurance, brings external business and community leadership, though she has not been assigned to any board committee and has no related-party transactions with the bank. Her concurrent roles on the boards of the Southeastern Lion Athletic Association and the Louisiana Children’s Discovery Center underscore her regional ties, potentially strengthening First Guaranty’s connections with local stakeholders and its community-focused profile.

The latest announcement is out from Delek US Holdings ( (DK) ).

At Delek US Holdings’ April 20, 2026 annual meeting, shareholders elected ten directors to serve until the 2027 meeting, reaffirming the existing board slate and governance continuity. Investors also approved on an advisory basis the company’s executive compensation program, signaling support for current pay practices for named executive officers.

Stockholders approved a new 2026 Long-Term Incentive Plan to replace the 2016 plan, ending further awards under the older program and updating Delek’s equity-based compensation framework. Shareholders additionally ratified Ernst & Young LLP as the independent auditor for fiscal 2026, maintaining the company’s external audit relationship and reinforcing financial oversight structures.

data.title: Webuild Publishes Updated Bylaws, Underscoring Governance Transparency
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Webuild SpA ( (IT:WBD) ) has provided an announcement.

Webuild S.p.A., the Italian-based infrastructure group recognized as a global leader in sustainable mobility, hydroelectric and water infrastructure, and green building, continues to leverage its 120-year track record across nearly 50 countries, supported by a €58.4 billion order backlog that is largely aligned with UN Sustainable Development Goals. Listed in Milan and included in the MIB ESG index, the company’s extensive portfolio spans flagship projects such as the Grand Ethiopian Renaissance Dam, the Panama Canal expansion, and key metro, rail, and highway corridors in Europe, the Americas, the Middle East, and Australia.

The company announced that its newly adopted corporate bylaws have been filed with the Companies Register of Milan – Monza Brianza – Lodi and are now publicly available through its website, registered office, and the 1Info authorized storage system. The publication of the updated bylaws signals a completed step in Webuild’s governance evolution and provides investors and other stakeholders with enhanced transparency on the group’s corporate framework, potentially reinforcing its positioning within ESG-focused capital markets and among global infrastructure partners.

The most recent analyst rating on (IT:WBD) stock is a Buy with a EUR3.40 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Webuild SpA stock, see the IT:WBD Stock Forecast page.

More about Webuild SpA

Webuild S.p.A. is a global leader in large and complex infrastructure construction, focusing on sustainable mobility, hydroelectric power, water management, and green building, and is a long-standing top player worldwide in water-sector infrastructure. Active in about 50 countries with roughly 95,000 people and more than 3,700 completed projects, it is listed on the Milan Stock Exchange and features in the MIB ESG index for its strong environmental, social, and governance profile.

Headquartered in Italy and directed by Salini Costruttori S.p.A., Webuild has built railways, metro lines, roads, bridges, tunnels, dams, and hydro plants, including landmark works such as the Grand Ethiopian Renaissance Dam, the Panama Canal expansion, and major metro systems in European and Middle Eastern capitals. As of 31 December 2025, it reported €13.6 billion in revenues and a €58.4 billion order backlog, with over 95% of construction activities aligned with United Nations Sustainable Development Goals, underscoring its strategic focus on sustainable infrastructure worldwide.

Technical Sentiment Signal: Hold

Current Market Cap: €2.6B

data.title: UBS Warns Swiss Capital Plan Could Add $37 Billion Burden After Credit Suisse Deal
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

ETRACS Alerian MLP Index ETN Class B ( (AMUB) ) has issued an announcement.

On 22 April 2026, UBS sharply criticized new Swiss regulatory capital measures after the Federal Council finalized changes to the Capital Adequacy Ordinance and sent proposed Banking Act amendments on foreign participations to parliament, arguing the package is extreme, not internationally aligned and potentially damaging to the Swiss economy. The revised rules will tighten capital treatment for capitalized software and prudential valuation adjustments from 2027–2029, cutting about USD 4 billion of CET1 at group level and USD 2 billion at UBS AG standalone, while a separate proposal to fully deduct foreign participations from CET1 over seven years would force UBS AG to hold roughly USD 20 billion in additional CET1, lifting total extra capital needs linked to the Credit Suisse deal to about USD 37 billion and driving annual capital costs of around USD 3 billion.

UBS said the Swiss government’s pro‑forma capital ratios and peer comparisons are misleading and warned that the combined impact of the measures would push its de facto minimum group CET1 ratio to about 18.4%, or 17.6% after CAO-related derecognition, further understating its capital strength versus rivals. Citing an independent BAK Economics study forecasting up to CHF 34 billion in lost Swiss GDP over ten years from the foreign participation rules, UBS questioned the government’s impact assessment but stressed it will maintain its 2026 financial targets and planned capital returns, remain committed to its diversified business model, and seek ways to protect shareholders while limiting adverse effects on clients and employees as the parliamentary process unfolds.

More about ETRACS Alerian MLP Index ETN Class B

UBS Group AG, headquartered in Zurich and Basel, is a global financial services provider and Switzerland’s largest bank, with a core focus on wealth management, domestic Swiss banking, asset management and a targeted investment banking franchise. Following its acquisition of Credit Suisse, UBS operates as a systemically important institution with a substantial international footprint and is subject to some of the world’s most stringent capital regulations.

Average Trading Volume: 612

data.title: Protector Forsikring Delivers Strong Premium Growth but Weaker Profits on Investment Losses in Q1 2026
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Protector Forsikring ASA ( (PSKRF) ) has issued an update.

Protector Forsikring ASA reported a strong top-line performance in the first quarter of 2026, with gross written premiums up 20% year-on-year, or 21% in local currencies, and a solid combined ratio of 84.9% indicating continued underwriting profitability. Growth was supported by key renewal dates, including a NOK 122m increase in UK gross written premiums on 1 April, underscoring the insurer’s strategic push in that market.

Despite the robust underwriting, results were hit by weaker investment performance, with total investment return including insurance finance at negative NOK 231m, contributing to a sharp drop in profit to NOK 165m and earnings per share of NOK 1.8. Even so, the company maintained a strong post-dividend solvency ratio of 220% and the board approved a dividend of NOK 659m, or NOK 8.00 per share, signaling confidence in capital strength and ongoing shareholder payouts.

The most recent analyst rating on (PSKRF) stock is a Hold with a NOK544.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Protector Forsikring ASA stock, see the PSKRF Stock Forecast page.

More about Protector Forsikring ASA

Protector Forsikring ASA is a Nordic insurance company focused on non-life insurance, offering products such as commercial and public sector property and casualty cover. The group has a growing presence in the UK market, aligning its premium inflows with key inception dates in both the Nordic region and the UK to drive international expansion.

data.title: Mips CFO Karin Rosenthal to Step Down After Six Years in Role
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An update from MIPS AB ( (SE:MIPS) ) is now available.

Mips AB, the Stockholm-listed safety technology group behind the Mips and Koroyd ingredient brands, develops science-based impact protection systems that are integrated into helmets and other protective products by major global manufacturers. Its technologies draw on more than 30 years of research and advanced aerospace safety science, reinforcing the company’s role as a specialist supplier in the premium protective equipment market.

The company announced that Chief Financial Officer Karin Rosenthal, who has held the role since 2020, will step down from her position on 22 October 2026 following a mutually agreed decision with the board. She will remain in post to support the immediate search for a successor and ensure an orderly handover, a transition the CEO framed as occurring as Mips enters a new phase of development, suggesting continuity in financial leadership during a strategically important period for the group and its stakeholders.

The most recent analyst rating on (SE:MIPS) stock is a Buy with a SEK540.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on MIPS AB stock, see the SE:MIPS Stock Forecast page.

More about MIPS AB

Mips Group is a global safety company that operates an ingredient-brand business model through its Koroyd and Mips brands, supplying advanced impact protection technologies to leading helmet and protective gear manufacturers worldwide. Headquartered in Stockholm and listed on Nasdaq Stockholm, the group leverages decades of research partnerships, including with the Swedish Royal Institute of Technology and Karolinska Institutet, to develop innovative helmet-based and aerospace-derived safety solutions.

Average Trading Volume: 125,101

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: SEK7.24B
